name: Antoni Gaudi
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Irabu Island
description: This dive site off Irabu Island features dramatic rock formations and swim-throughs, with strong currents attracting pelagic life. Expect schools of trevally, barracuda, and occasional reef sharks. The topography includes steep walls and overhangs covered in soft corals. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, making it ideal for drift diving. Best suited for experienced divers due to the challenging conditions.

name: Kuba Kita Isobana Course
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Kuba Island
description: This dive site off Kuba Island features a stunning coral reef with vibrant soft and hard corals. The gentle slope offers excellent visibility, often exceeding 20 meters, with frequent sightings of clownfish, butterflyfish, and occasional reef sharks. Currents are mild, making it ideal for all skill levels. The underwater topography includes small caves and overhangs teeming with marine life. Water temperatures stay warm year-round, averaging 26°C. The site's proximity to the East China Sea brings nutrient-rich waters that support a thriving ecosystem.

name: Nakachinshi
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Yakabi Island

name: Nagahama
description: Dive into clear Pacific waters off Okinawa, where vibrant coral gardens thrive along gentle slopes. Schools of tropical fish dart between formations, while eagle rays and reef sharks occasionally pass by. The site offers excellent visibility year-round, with mild currents ideal for all skill levels. Rocky outcrops create small swim-throughs and overhangs teeming with macro life. Depths range from shallow reefs to deeper walls, perfect for both snorkelers and divers. Water temperatures stay warm, making it a comfortable dive in any season.

name: Osaki The Mansion Of Long Finned Batfish
description: Near Ishigaki, this site features a stunning coral-covered pinnacle rising from the depths. Schools of longfin batfish swirl around the structure, creating a mesmerizing spectacle. The clear waters offer excellent visibility, with occasional reef sharks and turtles passing by. Strong currents can occur, making it ideal for drift diving. Vibrant soft corals and sea fans add color, while deeper sections reveal dramatic drop-offs. Best suited for intermediate to advanced divers due to variable conditions.

name: Ijyakajya
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Aka Island
description: The dive site off Aka Island features vibrant coral gardens teeming with tropical fish, including clownfish and butterflyfish. The sloping reef drops to sandy patches where you might spot garden eels. Visibility is excellent, often exceeding 30 meters, with mild currents ideal for drift diving. The area is known for occasional encounters with sea turtles and reef sharks. The underwater terrain includes small caves and overhangs, adding variety to the dive. Water temperatures stay warm year-round, making it suitable for all experience levels. The site is part of the Kerama Islands' protected waters, ensuring healthy marine ecosystems.

name: Cross Line
description: This site features a stunning underwater ridge with dramatic drop-offs and vibrant coral gardens. Schools of tropical fish swirl around the rocky formations, while eagle rays and reef sharks occasionally cruise by. The current can be strong, making it ideal for drift diving. Visibility is often excellent, revealing a thriving ecosystem. The mix of hard and soft corals creates a colorful backdrop, with occasional sightings of sea turtles. Best suited for intermediate to advanced divers due to the currents. The site offers both shallow and deep sections, providing variety for different experience levels.

name: Mishiragi
description: This dive site off Okinawa features dramatic underwater cliffs and caves teeming with marine life. Strong currents attract large schools of fish, including trevally and barracuda. The steep walls drop to over 30 meters, covered in colorful soft corals. Divers often spot reef sharks and turtles patrolling the blue. Visibility typically exceeds 20 meters, with water temperatures ranging from 22-28°C. The site offers thrilling drift dives along the current-swept formations. Advanced divers will appreciate the challenging conditions and abundant pelagics.

name: Unajinosachi
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Zamami Island
description: This dive site near Zamami Island offers stunning coral formations and clear waters teeming with marine life. Expect encounters with sea turtles, colorful reef fish, and occasional manta rays. The gentle currents make it suitable for divers of all levels. The vibrant coral gardens and excellent visibility create a mesmerizing underwater experience. Located in Okinawa's pristine waters, it's a must for those exploring Japan's marine biodiversity.

name: Ugan
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Zamami Island
description: This dive site off Zamami Island offers clear waters with excellent visibility, often exceeding 30 meters. The sloping reef features vibrant coral gardens teeming with tropical fish, including clownfish and butterflyfish. Look for sea turtles gliding past, as they frequent the area. Gentle currents make it ideal for divers of all levels. The underwater terrain includes small caves and overhangs, perfect for exploration. Warm water temperatures year-round enhance the experience. The site is part of the Kerama Islands' rich marine ecosystem, known for its biodiversity.

name: Red Marker
description: This dive site off Okinawa features a vibrant artificial reef marked by a red buoy. The structure attracts schools of tropical fish, including butterflyfish and damselfish, while occasional reef sharks patrol the area. Gentle currents make it ideal for beginners. The sandy bottom slopes to 18 meters, with scattered coral formations. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters in the clear East China Sea waters.

name: Maeda Point
description: Maeda Point offers stunning underwater cliffs and caves, with visibility often exceeding 30 meters. The site features dramatic drop-offs, swim-throughs, and vibrant coral gardens teeming with reef fish. Look for sea turtles, eagle rays, and occasional reef sharks. Gentle currents make it ideal for divers of all levels. The famous "Blue Cave" entrance creates mesmerizing light effects. Accessible from shore, it's a must-dive in Okinawa.

name: Small World
description: This site off Ishigaki Island features vibrant coral gardens teeming with tropical fish, sea turtles, and occasional reef sharks. The clear waters offer excellent visibility, often exceeding 30 meters. Gentle currents make it ideal for divers of all levels. The underwater terrain includes swim-throughs and small caves, adding excitement to the dive. The area is known for its rich biodiversity, with frequent sightings of colorful nudibranchs and schools of anthias. Warm water temperatures year-round enhance the experience.

name: Naji / Kakurene
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Amuro Island

name: Kadena Seawall
description: This shore dive in Okinawa offers easy access with a gentle slope. The sandy bottom transitions to vibrant coral formations teeming with tropical fish. Look for octopus, moray eels, and occasional sea turtles. Currents are mild, making it ideal for beginners. The shallow depth and clear waters provide excellent visibility. Nearby rock formations create interesting swim-throughs. Night dives reveal nocturnal creatures like crabs and shrimp. The site is protected from strong waves, ensuring calm conditions year-round.

name: Ginowan Pinnacles
description: A stunning underwater pinnacle system off Okinawa, teeming with marine life. Dramatic rock formations rise from the deep, covered in colorful soft corals and sponges. Schools of tropical fish swirl around the structures while eagle rays and reef sharks patrol the area. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich waters, supporting exceptional biodiversity. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, revealing the full scale of these towering formations. The site offers both shallow coral gardens and deep drop-offs for advanced divers. Night dives reveal a different world of nocturnal creatures emerging from the reef.

name: Agaiushi
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Amuro Island

name: Hamanaka
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Agenashiku Island
description: This dive site off Agenashiku Island features stunning coral gardens teeming with colorful reef fish, sea turtles, and occasional reef sharks. The gentle current makes it ideal for drift diving along sloping reefs and small caves. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, revealing vibrant soft corals and anemones. The sandy bottom near the drop-off attracts garden eels and stingrays. Warm waters and minimal surge create excellent conditions for photographers and beginners. Night dives reveal octopus, crustaceans, and bioluminescent plankton. The site offers both shallow areas and deeper sections reaching 25 meters.

name: Yonaguni Monument/ Underwater Ruins
description: Dive into mysterious underwater formations off Yonaguni, where massive stone terraces and stair-like structures spark debate about their origin—natural or man-made. Strong currents attract schools of hammerheads, while the dramatic rock faces host vibrant corals and reef fish. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, revealing the site's eerie grandeur. Best for advanced divers due to depth and currents.

name: The Sea God's Palace
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Taketomi Island, Aragusuku Island

name: Gina
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Aka Island

name: Komonshikoro Coral
description: This vibrant coral garden in Ishigaki dazzles with colorful soft corals and abundant marine life. Schools of tropical fish dart between the formations, while eagle rays and turtles often glide past. The gentle current makes it ideal for drift diving. Visibility is excellent, often exceeding 30 meters. The site slopes from 5 to 20 meters, perfect for all skill levels. The coral bommies create stunning underwater landscapes. Night dives reveal a different world of nocturnal creatures. Warm waters year-round add to the appeal.

name: Hedo Misaki
description: Off Okinawa's northern tip, this site offers dramatic cliffs plunging into deep blue. Strong currents attract pelagics like tuna and reef sharks. The rocky terrain is dotted with swim-throughs and overhangs, home to vibrant soft corals and schools of tropical fish. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. Best for experienced divers due to challenging conditions. Winter brings cooler water but fewer crowds.

name: Furuzamami
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Zamami Island

name: Uss Longshaw
description: Off Okinawa’s coast lies a WWII wreck resting at 30 meters, encrusted with vibrant corals. Schools of batfish and trevally circle the intact structure, while lionfish and morays hide in the shadows. Strong currents attract pelagics, making it ideal for advanced divers. The deck guns and propeller remain visible, offering a haunting glimpse into history. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters, with occasional thermoclines adding challenge. Best dived in calm conditions.

name: Yona Tunnel
description: A submerged tunnel off Okinawa, this site offers a thrilling swim-through with dramatic light beams filtering through cracks. The structure is covered in soft corals and attracts schools of tropical fish. Strong currents can occur, making it ideal for drift diving. Depth ranges from 10 to 20 meters, suitable for intermediate divers. The surrounding area features vibrant reef life, including clownfish and occasional reef sharks. Visibility is typically excellent, enhancing the tunnel's eerie beauty.

name: Kabira Bay
description: Kabira Bay offers crystal-clear waters with excellent visibility, perfect for spotting sea turtles and vibrant coral gardens. The shallow, sheltered bay is ideal for beginners, with gentle currents and sandy patches between coral formations. Black pearl farms dot the area, adding a unique cultural touch. Snorkeling here is just as rewarding as diving, thanks to the abundant marine life and calm conditions. The bay's emerald hues and white sand make it a stunning underwater playground.

name: Maeda Flats
description: A gentle slope leads to vibrant coral gardens teeming with clownfish, parrotfish, and sea turtles. The clear, shallow entry makes it ideal for beginners, while deeper sections reveal intriguing rock formations. Currents are mild, visibility often exceeds 30 meters, and sunlight dances through the water, creating a magical atmosphere. Night dives reveal octopus and crustaceans. Easy shore access adds convenience.

name: Hamashima East Coral Garden
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Taketomi Island

name: Nishibama
region: Asia, Japan, Kyushu, Okinawa, Aka Island
description: A shallow coral garden near Aka Island, Okinawa, teeming with colorful reef fish and vibrant soft corals. Gentle currents make it ideal for beginners and snorkelers. The sandy bottom slopes to 12 meters, with occasional sea turtles gliding past. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters, revealing intricate coral formations. Night dives showcase nocturnal creatures like octopus and crustaceans. Accessible by boat from nearby islands, the site offers calm conditions year-round.

name: Ikei Island
description: A shallow, sandy dive site off Okinawa with vibrant coral gardens and abundant marine life. Ideal for beginners and macro lovers, the area teems with nudibranchs, gobies, and octopus. Gentle currents and excellent visibility make it a relaxed dive. The sloping reef transitions to a sandy bottom, where garden eels sway in the current. Nearby seagrass beds attract turtles. Warm waters year-round.

name: Sakieda Big Arch
description: A stunning underwater arch near Ishigaki, Okinawa, with clear blue waters and strong currents. The arch itself is massive, covered in colorful soft corals and sponges. Schools of trevally and barracuda often patrol the area, while reef sharks and turtles are common sightings. The site offers dramatic topography with swim-throughs and overhangs. Best for experienced divers due to currents. Visibility is excellent, often exceeding 30 meters. The arch sits at around 18 meters depth, with surrounding reefs teeming with tropical fish.
